Name: MindLogger
column11: Mobile apps (iOS/Android) + web app talking to a backend that can be self-hosted via Docker, or used through Child Mind Institute-hosted infrastructure
column12: English now, French/Spanish rolling out; designed for community translation
column13: Mental-health/behavioral surveys plus built-in cognitive tasks: Flanker, CPT (Continuous Performance Test), A/B Trail Making, plus drawing/audio/photo/GPS item types for custom tasks

Name: Concerto Platform
Hosting: Yes — purpose-built for online adaptive testing
Administration Language: Self-hosted on your own web server (Apache/Rack-style + R + MySQL stack) or via paid managed hosting at concertoplatform.com/aws
column11: Not a fixed battery — a platform for building/hosting adaptive tests (IRT-based)

Name: IAT implementations (multiple independent repos)
GitHub: https://github.com/winteram/IAT (MIT), https://github.com/theunvarnished/EasyIAT (no license file declared), https://github.com/tomstafford/IAT (GPL-2.0), https://github.com/ianhussey/ImplicitAssociationTest
Language: JS/HTML or PsychoPy (Python), varies by repo
Configurability: Easy (EasyIAT is config-file driven for single/multi-target IATs)
License: Varies by repo — see GitHub links
Online Administration: Yes — all are JS/HTML or PsychoPy-based and run in a browser
Hosting: Self-hosted (open the HTML/JS on any web server) or embedded into survey tools like Qualtrics via MinnoJS
Administration Language: Author-defined; stimuli/instructions are just text/image lists
Tasks Available: Implicit Association Test only (various sub-types: brief
